QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_64

question.text: If the majority of issue proceeds (excluding offer for sale component) are used for capital expenditure, what is the minimum lock-in period for the promoters' minimum contribution?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When the majority of issue proceeds (excluding OFS) are used for capital expenditure, the minimum lock-in period for promoters' minimum contribution is 3 years (not the standard 18 months). The excess promoter holding is locked for 1 year (not the standard 6 months). **The Trap:** 18 months is the standard lock-in for promoter minimum contribution, which is what most candidates will default to. The capex-heavy usage triggers the extended lock-in, reflecting the longer gestation period of capital projects. **Cross-Exam Context:** Lock-in periods are a core NISM Series IX topic. NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) may test understanding of lock-in as it affects share availability for trading. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The extended lock-in for capex-heavy IPOs aligns promoter commitment with the longer time horizon needed for capital projects to generate returns — it signals skin in the game.
